source_documents: https://www.fatf-gafi.org (Virtual Assets Red Flag Indicators, Sept 2020)
category: AML - laundering through virtual assets and VASPs
risk_signals: Structured fiat-to-crypto purchases across multiple exchanges to stay under thresholds
Use of mixers/tumblers, privacy coins, or chain-hopping to break traceability
Wallet exposure to darknet markets, scams, or sanctioned addresses
analyst_reasoning: Virtual assets enable fast, cross-border, pseudonymous value transfer. Launderers convert illicit fiat into crypto, then obscure the trail with mixers, privacy coins, and chain-hopping before cashing out. FATF's red-flag framework centres on obfuscation techniques and counterparty exposure rather than the transaction size alone.
investigation_steps: Trace on-chain flows and screen counterparties for darknet, scam, or sanctioned exposure
Detect mixer/tumbler use, privacy-coin conversion, and chain-hopping between assets
Correlate fiat on-ramp/off-ramp activity at VASPs with structuring patterns
decision: File an STR, restrict the VASP relationship, and use blockchain-analytics attribution to support the report.
outcome: Once mixed or bridged, crypto proceeds are difficult to attribute; on-ramp/off-ramp monitoring at VASPs is the key control point.
lessons_learned: Obfuscation techniques (mixers, privacy coins, chain-hopping) are the core crypto-ML red flags
Fiat on-ramps and off-ramps at VASPs are the practical monitoring choke points
Counterparty (address) exposure matters more than transaction size on-chain
